name: Following the Water Trail
name_original: Senderos del Agua y de las Letanias — circular desde Ea
description: Hike the moderate 8.1-mile Water and Litanies Paths circular from Ea, featuring a Roman bridge and a hermitage.
region_intro: Hiking trails Ea offer a diverse landscape characterized by a coastal setting where mountains meet the sea. The region features a ria extending inland, a creek, and small stone bridges, providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. The surrounding area includes mountainous roads and the nearby Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, contributing to its natural heritage. This combination of coastal and inland features makes Ea a compelling destination for hikers. Best hiking trails around Ea The most popular hiking route is Following the Water Trail, an 8.1 miles (13.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 53 minutes to complete, tracing a path through the local landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Laga Beach – View from San Pedro de Atxarre loop from Ibarrangelu, a moderate 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path offering views of the coastline and surrounding natural areas. Local hikers also love the Talaia (276 m) – Mount Ogoño (305 m) loop from Ibarrangelu, a 4.3 miles (7.0 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Ea is defined by coastal paths, mountainous regions, and a unique ria landscape. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate coastal walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Ea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 850 reviews. More than 5,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Ea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Ea, and what are their difficulty levels?
A: There are over 220 hiking routes around Ea. These include a variety of options: 74 easy trails, 118 moderate trails, and 28 difficult trails, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes near Ea?
A: Yes, Ea offers several circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14993756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Ea – Ea loop from Ea&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.7-mile (10.8 km) trail that incorporates the historic Roman Bridge. Another option is the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508352" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia (276 m) – Mount Ogoño (305 m) loop from Ibarrangelu&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.3 miles (7.0 km) long and leads through mountainous terrain.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking around Ea?
A: Ea's trails offer views of its unique natural setting, including a ria extending inland, a creek, and small stone bridges. You can explore highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3921343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Artzubi Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also close to the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, offering diverse landscapes. Hikers can also find viewpoints such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from San Pedro de Atxarre&lt;/a&gt; and summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2868489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Ogoño (305 m)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there easy or family-friendly hiking options in Ea?
A: Yes, Ea has 74 easy trails suitable for families or those looking for a relaxed walk. The village's lagoon-like beach and the general charm of the area make it pleasant for all ages. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, the abundance of easy trails suggests many gentle paths to explore the local landscape.

Q: Can I hike with my dog around Ea?
A: Many trails in the Ea region are suitable for hiking with dogs, provided they are kept under control and local regulations are followed. It's always a good idea to check specific trail rules or signage for any restrictions, especially in protected areas like the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking in Ea?
A: Given Ea's coastal location in the Basque Country, spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be warm, while winter might bring cooler, wetter weather, though coastal walks can still be enjoyable.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Ea?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Ea, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 850 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape where mountains meet the sea, the unique ria, and the charming village setting. The variety of trails, from coastal paths to mountainous ascents, is also a frequently mentioned highlight.

Q: Are there any longer or more challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, there are 28 difficult trails around Ea. A notable longer route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38197555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laga Beach – View from San Pedro de Atxarre loop from Ibarrangelu&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 10.1-mile (16.3 km) path offering extensive coastal views and taking around 4 hours 54 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a water source or river?
A: Yes, one of the notable options in the area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1046023772" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Following the Water Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 8.1-mile (13.1 km) route traces a path through the local landscape, likely incorporating the creek and small stone bridges characteristic of Ea's natural setting.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Ea?
A: Hiking around Ea offers a diverse range of terrain. You can expect coastal paths where mountains meet the sea, trails along a ria that extends inland, paths alongside a creek with small stone bridges, and more rugged mountainous sections. The roads leading to Ea are also described as mountainous, indicating varied topography.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or summits accessible by hiking in Ea?
A: Absolutely. The region features several points offering panoramic views. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4006662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from San Pedro de Atxarre&lt;/a&gt; or ascend to summits like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2868489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Ogoño (305 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4687497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia (276 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of popular loop trails.
tips:
Small town on the coast of Vizcaya (Spain). Since the 16th century, fishing has been the most important traditional activity.
Ea has a river that divides the town into two parts. The houses are located around this stream, forming two long parallel streets. There are 4 bridges that make it easy for us to move from one street to another. One of the bridges, the so-called 'Roman bridge', has become the image of the town and its image appears on the municipality's flag.

Also known as Ermita de la Concepción and Ermita de la Atalaya is a religious building in Spain. It is located in the Biscayan town of Ea. The hermitage is located in a natural watchtower, which is accessed from a small path located in the port of the town.
One of the three main districts of Eako (Bizkaia), located to the east of the municipality. It had its own municipality until the 1887 census. The popular pronunciation of the name is "Bedaru".

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Water and Litanies Paths trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While the total distance is around 13 km with approximately 400 meters of elevation gain, the specific terrain can vary. Moderate trails typically involve some uneven surfaces and sustained ascents, requiring a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: Where does the Water and Litanies Paths trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is circular and starts from Ea. While specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, Ea is a small coastal village, and you can usually find public parking areas within or near the village center. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Is the Water and Litanies Paths trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Generally, many trails in the Basque Country are dog-friendly, especially if kept on a leash. Given the nature of this hike, it's likely suitable for well-behaved dogs. Always ensure you carry water for your pet and clean up after them. Check local signage at the trailhead for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail, like many coastal routes in the Basque Country, is during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is typically milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be pleasant but may be warmer and more crowded, while winter can bring more rain and cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes by several interesting spots. You'll encounter the historic Roman Bridge of Ea, the charming Talako Ama Hermitage, and the small settlement of Bedarona. These offer opportunities to appreciate local history and culture.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on the Water and Litanies Paths?
faqs.answer: As the name suggests, the trail likely features elements related to water, which often means diverse and captivating scenery. You can anticipate views that might include coastal landscapes, possibly river or stream sections, and the lush greenery typical of the Basque Country. Trails near water often showcase vibrant flora and unique geological features.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ura jarraituz (63% of the route), Letaniak bidezidorra (45%), and Bizkaiko bira (20%) trails, among others like Ikuspegiez gozatuz and Olagorta auzoa. This means you'll experience a blend of different local paths.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, typically, hiking trails in this region of the Basque Country, especially those accessible from villages like Ea, do not require permits or entrance fees. You are free to enjoy the natural beauty without any special permissions.

faqs.question: Is public transport an option to reach the trailhead in Ea?
faqs.answer: Ea is a small village, and while specific public transport links directly to the trailhead are not detailed, it is generally accessible by local bus services from larger towns in the Basque Country. It's recommended to check current bus schedules to Ea if you plan to use public transport.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a moderate hike like this?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike of this length (around 13 km), essential items include comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket or rain gear, as coastal weather can change quickly. A basic first-aid kit and a fully charged phone are also advisable.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the Water and Litanies Paths trail?
faqs.answer: Ea is a small village, but it typically offers a few local cafes, restaurants, and guesthouses or rural accommodations. You can find options for refreshments or an overnight stay within the village itself, especially during tourist seasons. It's a good idea to check availability in advance.
